text:
- **Time-relative strings**: `relative_time()` (util, ~line 68) buckets into `"just now"` (<60s), `"N min ago"` (<1h), `"N hour(s) ago"` (<24h), `"yesterday"` (<48h), `"N days ago"`. Re-rendering the same underlying state a few seconds later can silently change `"just now"` → `"1 min ago"`, or flip a message from included to `omitted` as the 4h window slides. **Not diffable/replayable without embedding wall-clock `now`.**
- **Shape flip on cursor state**: the single most consequential nondeterminism — whether `<members>`/`<subchannels>` vs. `<recent-presence>` render at all is gated by `cursor == 0`, and `cursor` is mutated by the very act of rendering (`set_seen_cursor`) and by a **compare-and-swap race** across concurrent `PostToolUse` hooks (`turns.rs:135-145`, "only the first of any concurrent PostToolUse hooks wins the CAS; the rest get `delta_since=None` and emit nothing"). Two parallel tool calls in the same turn can nondeterministically produce different injected context depending on which hook process wins the race.
